skill.name: reasoning-abductive
skill.description: Generate and evaluate explanatory hypotheses from incomplete observations. Use when diagnosing anomalies, explaining unexpected outcomes, or inferring causes from effects.
skill.summary: Rank likely explanations for surprising observations using evidence, confidence, and next-step tests.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but contextual review found only Markdown examples, ordinary prose, and structured reasoning templates in SKILL.md. No executable commands, prompt injection attempts, network access, filesystem access, credential handling, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: Static external command findings dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution matches are Markdown code fence delimiters and example blocks. They are documentation text, not executable commands or instructions to run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited line is a Markdown code fence or part of a fenced example. There is no executable script, command invocation, or user-input command construction in the skill.

low_findings.title: Static weak cryptography findings dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography matches occur in ordinary description text, type signatures, headings, or business examples. No cryptographic API, hashing function, encryption routine, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context contains reasoning methodology and example observations only. I did not find evidence of MD5, DES, SHA-1, or any other cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Static reconnaissance findings dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance matches are normal diagnostic questions in a reasoning guide. They do not instruct the agent to enumerate hosts, collect system details, scan networks, or gather local environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are conceptual diagnostic prompts about explaining observations and identifying time boundaries. No system or network reconnaissance command or workflow is present.

content.value_statement: Teams often face incomplete data when explaining anomalies, failures, or unexpected outcomes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured hypothesis generation, evidence scoring, and ranked explanations.
actual_capabilities: Structures raw observations into clear anomaly descriptions.
Generates multiple plausible explanations across technical, product, market, operational, and external categories.
Scores hypotheses using explanatory power, simplicity, coherence, testability, and prior probability.
Ranks candidate explanations with confidence levels and supporting evidence.
Identifies contradicting evidence, uncertainty, and practical next steps.
limitations: It does not collect evidence automatically from external systems.
Its conclusions depend on the quality and completeness of provided observations.
It cannot prove causality without follow-up tests or additional data.
It may produce several plausible explanations when evidence is sparse.

use_cases.title: Diagnose product metric drops
use_cases.description: Explain changes in conversion, activation, retention, or revenue by comparing competing hypotheses against available evidence.

use_cases.title: Investigate operational anomalies
use_cases.description: Turn unexpected process failures or performance changes into ranked causes and practical verification steps.

use_cases.title: Analyze incident symptoms
use_cases.description: Generate likely explanations for system behavior from logs, timelines, impact scope, and known changes.

prompt_templates.title: Explain an anomaly
prompt_templates.prompt: Use abductive reasoning to explain this observation: [describe anomaly]. Generate at least five hypotheses and rank the top three.

prompt_templates.title: Compare possible causes
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these observations and evidence: [data]. Score each hypothesis for explanatory power, simplicity, coherence, testability, and prior probability.

prompt_templates.title: Find missing evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use abductive reasoning on this issue: [issue]. Identify the strongest explanations, contradicting evidence, missing evidence, and tests to reduce uncertainty.

prompt_templates.title: Build a full diagnostic brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete abductive reasoning brief for [case]. Include anomaly framing, diverse hypotheses, evidence scoring, ranked conclusions, confidence bounds, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Activation fell from 38 percent to 24 percent after a signup redesign.
output: Most likely explanation: the redesign introduced extra friction during account setup.
Supporting evidence: the drop begins after launch and affects new users more than returning users.
Next step: compare step-level completion rates before and after the redesign.

output_examples.input: Enterprise deals closed slower in Q4, while pipeline volume stayed stable.
output: Top hypothesis: sales cycle elongation rather than demand loss.
Alternative explanations: stakeholder turnover, pricing review delays, or competitor displacement.
Missing evidence: stage aging, procurement timelines, and lost-deal reasons.

best_practices: Provide concrete observations, timeframes, baselines, and affected segments.
Include both supporting and contradicting evidence for each major hypothesis.
Treat the top explanation as provisional until follow-up tests confirm it.
anti_patterns: Do not stop after the first plausible explanation.
Do not use confidence scores when evidence is missing or vague.
Do not treat abductive reasoning as proof of causality.

faq.question: What is abductive reasoning?
faq.answer: Abductive reasoning identifies the best explanation for observations when the available evidence is incomplete.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when you need to explain anomalies, failures, metric changes, or unexpected outcomes.

faq.question: Does this skill prove root cause?
faq.answer: No. It ranks likely explanations and recommends tests that can confirm or reject them.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the observation, baseline, timing, scope, known changes, and any supporting or contradicting evidence.

faq.question: Can it handle technical incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize symptoms, timelines, system changes, and evidence into ranked incident explanations.

faq.question: Is this safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed skill is documentation-only and does not contain executable commands or unsafe data access.
